factors.summary: The company has not provided any updated forward guidance, as there have been no material changes in their risk factors.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes from our risk factors as disclosed in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2015.

factors.summary: Demand is mixed across different segments.

evidence.text: Revenue in the Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment decreased in the current year period due to reduced levels of project execution activities in the mining and metals business line and for certain large upstream projects progressing to completion. Revenue growth in the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment due to increased project execution activities for several power projects, as well as revenue contributions from the Stork acquisition, largely offset this revenue decline.

factors.summary: Backlog is growing, particularly in the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment.

evidence.text: Consolidated backlog as of March 31, 2016 was $46.0 billion compared to $41.2 billion as of March 31, 2015. The increase in backlog was primarily due to significant new awards booked after the first quarter of 2015 in the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Maintenance, Modification & Asset Integrity segment post-acquisition.

evidence.text: Revenue for the Maintenance, Modification & Asset Integrity segment increased by 24 percent compared to the three months ended March 31, 2015. Revenue in 2016 includes one month of revenue associated with the recent acquisition of the Stork business, which closed on March 1, 2016.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed, with some segments experiencing deceleration while others show growth.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth is mixed; for example, the Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment saw a 18% decrease in revenue, while the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment saw a 52% increase.

factors.summary: Volume trends vary by segment, with some areas showing declines and others growth.

evidence.text: Revenue for the Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment decreased by 18% compared to the prior year period due to reduced project execution activities in mining and metals. In contrast, the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment saw a 52% increase in revenue due to increased project execution activities.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are in downstream petrochemical and select upstream markets, while mining and metals are slowing.

evidence.text: The Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment remains well positioned for new project activity in downstream, petrochemical and select upstream markets; however, declining oil prices since the latter part of 2014 have affected the timing of new awards and the pace of execution on certain existing projects. The mining and metals business has continued to slow as major capital investment decisions by most mining customers have been deferred.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are stable or slightly declining in some segments.

evidence.text: Segment profit margin for the three months ended March 2016 is essentially level with the corresponding period in 2015. The segment profit margin decreased to 6.5% for the Maintenance, Modification & Asset Integrity segment compared to 9.0% in the prior year.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, particularly in the Maintenance, Modification & Asset Integrity segment.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures of $49 million and $74 million for the three months ended March 31, 2016 and 2015, respectively, primarily related to construction equipment associated with equipment operations in the Maintenance, Modification & Asset Integrity segment, as well as expenditures for facilities and investments in information technology.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are increasing, indicating investment in growth areas.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing, with the company taking on more debt to finance acquisitions and other activities.

evidence.text: The company's total debt to total capitalization ratio as of March 31, 2016 was 35.4% compared to 24.8% as of December 31, 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage is rising, posing a financial risk.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its dividend but reducing stock buybacks.

evidence.text: Cash utilized by financing activities during the three months ended March 31, 2016 included the repurchase and cancellation of 202,650 shares of the company's common stock for $10 million under its stock repurchase program. Quarterly cash dividends of $0.21 per share were declared in the first quarter of 2016 and 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Dividends are maintained, but buybacks are reduced, showing mixed signals.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities was $115 million for the three months ended March 31, 2016 compared to $39 million for the corresponding period of the prior year. The improvement in cash flows from operating activities resulted primarily from cash outflows in the 2015 period totaling $306 million associated with discontinued operations as discussed below, partially offset by unfavorable period-over-period changes in working capital.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving free cash flow suggests better financial health.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are active and creating value, particularly the Stork acquisition.

evidence.text: On March 1, 2016, the company acquired 100% of Stork Holding B.V. for an aggregate purchase price of 695 million (or approximately $756 million), including the assumption of debt and other liabilities. The operations of Stork will be reported in the Maintenance, Modification & Asset Integrity segment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active acquisitions, like Stork, indicate strategic growth.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to risks in the market and economic conditions.

evidence.text: The company wishes to caution readers that forward-looking statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties which could cause actual results of operations to differ materially from expectations. Factors potentially contributing to such differences include, among others: The cyclical nature of many of the markets the company serves, including our commodity-based business lines, and our vulnerability to downturns; Current economic conditions affecting our clients, partners, subcontractors and suppliers, which may result in decreased capital investment or expenditures, or a failure to make anticipated increased capital investment or expenditures, by the company's clients or other financial difficulties by our partners, subcontractors or suppliers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management caution due to market risks.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include cyclical market downturns, economic conditions affecting clients, and cybersecurity breaches.

evidence.text: Factors potentially contributing to such differences include, among others: The cyclical nature of many of the markets the company serves, including our commodity-based business lines, and our vulnerability to downturns; Current economic conditions affecting our clients, partners, subcontractors and suppliers, which may result in decreased capital investment or expenditures, or a failure to make anticipated increased capital investment or expenditures, by the company's clients or other financial difficulties by our partners, subcontractors or suppliers; Cybersecurity breaches of our systems and information technology.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to commodity prices, economic conditions, and currency risks.

evidence.text: Factors potentially contributing to such differences include, among others: Current economic conditions affecting our clients, partners, subcontractors and suppliers, which may result in decreased capital investment or expenditures, or a failure to make anticipated increased capital investment or expenditures, by the company's clients or other financial difficulties by our partners, subcontractors or suppliers; Foreign exchange risks; The potential impact of certain tax matters including, but not limited to, those from foreign operations and the ongoing audits by tax authorities; The impact of anti-bribery and international trade laws and regulations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: High sensitivity to various external factors.

factors.summary: New awards and successful project execution are key catalysts for growth.

evidence.text: Consolidated new awards were $4.7 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2016 compared to new awards of $4.4 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2015. The Government and Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segments were the major contributors to the new award activity in the first quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The increase in new awards and successful project execution indicates growth opportunities.
